metadata.title:
STRIKE ON
|. /. f. N. LEAD


BE- wv

pages.raw_text:
kn News of probably as great a
mining strike as has been reported
from the Occur dAlene district in
recent years has been received by
the officers of the H. e. M. Co.,
whose property is located on Dago
peak, about three miles from Wal
lace, and on the Burke formation.


This formation is a particularly
great one, as the most of the large
Occur d'Alene properties are on it..
News Of the strike was received
yesterday by Collins brothers, who
are among the heaviest stockhold-
ers., Other heavy stockholders are
c. L. Drum, c. A. Montgomery and
H. V. Elders of Wallace.


Work on the property consists
of two tunnels. In the upper a
large body of low grade and some
high grade ore was struck. 1t was
calculated to run the lower tunnel
$50 feet to cut the ledge, but this
was accomplished in So feet, which
is 400 feet west of the showing
above.


Samples sent to Spokane assay
about $50 to the ton, about 20 per
cent lead and from $40 to $50 in
silver.


The M. E. M. owns group of II
claims about midway between the
famous Hercules and Bunker Hill
& Sullivan mines. The capitaliza-
ton of the company is $1,000 900,
100000 of which is treasury stock.
There are no shares for sale.


The ledge is a tissue in Burke
quartzite, with trend slightly
south of east The formation and
surface groupings compare favor
ably with the producing mines of
the district. The strike" is 26
inches wide, all shipping ore. No
machinery is on the property yet.
kThe property is located within ex-
-cellent shipping facilities, being a
Emile and half from the o. r. &
N, with almost a water grade.
